I wasn't familiar with the course but had a feeling from the name that the monster hill was looming ahead the whole time

A YouTuber (GPLama) analyzed the course on best bike split and has suggested the fastest possible strategy is reverse-splits in the sense that your power for the flat portion should be less than the ride average, while the power for the climb should be above it.    I had NO warm up today (5am start lol) but I think I can take another 30 seconds off by putting down a little more power at the start without letting the heart rate go nuts.

Essentially on this ride I did 233 watts (90% FTP) on the flat ~6 km and 297W (114%) on the climb
The interesting thing, is the time elapsed for both sections is going to be almost identical. Ride average is right around 100% FTP.

Next time I'll try to average just a few watts higher and repeat as I gain fitness.  Or die.

I know it’s easy to be hard on yourself when you have big goals in mind, but I want you to know that seeing you out there struggling in the same way that I was gave me determination to press on. I wasn’t supposed to be at this race this year, but SRR gave me that opportunity and I was not going to let them down. Whenever you’d catch up to where I was at it gave me that reminder that I would NOT quit. Considering we qualified at the very same race with very similar times and got to spend a good part of the day together. I can say that you were a big influence on my experience here today and I think a lot of other people can say the same thing.  Not giving up is more admirable in my opinion than cruising fast miles and throwing in the towel when things turn for the worse.  Especially coming back from injury this year  you showed true grit today and are still an inspiration to the entire club.  Thanks for the long runs and laughs and we will be back
